name: Oooh, clockwise!
description: Saw a massive coyote, hazed it, then almost fell into a small ravine while looking at it sideways and running forwards.
Rode a bike. saw a mule deer.

name: Stanley Park Bike Cruise
description: Sea lion sighting

name: Vancouver - Stanley Park
description: Last day of vacation 😭

name: Nanaimo - Neck Point Park
description: forgot to start the recording but we did this. Just over a mile. Sea lions and fairy houses https://www.alltrails.com/trail/canada/british-columbia/neck-point-park-loop

name: Seattle 2 - Green Lake
description: "slow run club" with Emmet which actually involves running sorta fast and then walking for a bit and then running at a medium pace the rest of the way. Beautiful day with a view of Mt. Rainier

name: Seattle 1
description: Squeezing in a quick sanity jog before more sightseeing. Saw a Massachusetts "low number" license plate (it's a strange lottery option) with an MIT parking sticker on it.

name: Kids climbing chaos
description: Met up with other friends in Bellevue. Turns out their kids are also maximum hype. Climbing long rope walls and topping out on boulders. Also quite a bit of yelling

name: Child meander
description: Proof of vacation. Met up with Anna de Regt. Kids rode the salmon slide

name: Lunch Pickleball
description: The Pacific Highlands Ranch courts were full, proving that everybody slacks off in San Diego. (We were also meeting another fully employed friend.) Luckily the high school was on spring break so we got to use their PE courts.

name: Afternoon Mountain Bike Ride
description: I got Joel Brooks an action cam for his birthday so that I can make him take videos of me. Here's a snippet that explains while these trails are all named Tunnels  PS: everyone slept the whole night last night

name: Afternoon Mountain Bike Ride
description: I'm back to complain about our children's sleep. This time mostly the other one  11:45 pm, 2 am, 3:30 am, 4:30 am, alarm at 5:40 am  Joel Brooks and I decided flat ground was a good call  Note: sticks are snakes until proven otherwise

name: I am (not) Moana
description: Day date with Joel Brooks ! Absolutely gorgeous day to be on the ocean. However, ocean also makes for very difficult SUPping. Quads are burning already. Not like putzing along the Chattahoochee (our only other experience, 7+ years ago). Also don't sleep on the physicality of hauling the boards up the hill from the beach

name: Afternoon Pickleball
description: Played singles with Joel. Got some good rallies in.  Should I have been working? Maybe. But AI is doing my job, right?

name: Post drop off
description: S likes to go to school early and get school breakfast on Fridays (they have breakfast everyday but she only wants it on Fridays) so I was able to sneak in a run before my next meeting and while the fog was still overhead.  Happen to be wearing shorts that said MIT on and then I bumped in into a man wearing an MIT shirt! Class of 79.  Still have a nagging hamstring twinge. Only shows up while running.  Saw an awesome big male quail.

name: Practice makes... Marginally better
description: Two laps of a decently challenging trail. Definitely got better the second time around. I just kept yelling at myself "bend yah ahms" in a thick Boston accent in my head. Unclear why that's effective

name: Afternoon Pickleball
description: I think I'm getting better? Warmer than it seemed

name: Safety bail
description: Was excited to have cooler weather and Joel's turn to do swim class. Alas, terrible sleep rears its ugly head again (I even forgot my sunglasses 🤦🏻‍♀️). Planned to do Black Widow but after making a ton of stupid mistakes on the easy trails, decided that was unwise. Maybe when M turns 4 she'll miraculously start sleeping better

name: Morning Run
description: Was able to get out early and benefit from the surprisingly cool air that sinks down toward the creek overnight. Not gonna be pleasant today

name: Pre heat wave
description: Technically the heat advisory is already on but today was not so bad. A shocking number of people on the trails given the temperature and time of day though. Also lots of quail but they are too skittish for pics.  Original plan was to climb T4 and go down T2 and stay in the shade, but I realized that I was leaving the house right when school was letting out and I had to go the other way to avoid being spotted.   One little wash out on loose rocks at the bottom of barrel roll but managed to land on my feet.

name: Morning Run
description: Had a niggling butt twinge for the first 10 min and a lot of overall stiffness. I guess I'm not used to running in the mornings anymore.

name: Lunch Mountain Bike Ride
description: Joel and I explored a new area. They keep the map kind of hush hush but we managed to find a guy who gave us a few tips. Unfortunately he could not make us magically better bikers. Definitely some difficult trails

name: Lifting at the Y
description: Had an awkward time between my dentist appointment and picking up kiddo so I figured I'd actually try out the YMCA that we are members of. Used the outdoor lifting area! First time lifting in like a year, I surely won't feel that tomorrow

name: Afternoon Pickleball
description: Doubles

name: Benign snake
description: Roughly the same spot, but definitely a gopher snake this time. 'tis the season, I guess

name: Ooh that's nature
description: Coyote spotting on Side Hill, maybe the same one Joel Brooks saw a while back. Then my heart exploded when I saw what I'm pretty sure was a rattlesnake. Literally screamed. It was thick

name: Cul-de-sac Pickleball
description: Chalked out some lines. It works

name: Well F that
description: Finally had a "lunch" break at 2:45. Heat wave. That was a hungry, sweaty run

name: Splish splash
description: Joel, to some other biker: yeah, the puddle is pretty deep, I walked around Other biker, looking at me: *she* didn't   🤷🏻‍♀️

name: Belly Full of Banh Mi
description: Saw a gopher snake, maybe 3ft long  A bunch of breathless 10 year old boys also warned us about seeing two rattlesnakes, but I dunno. Also they were riding e bikes without helmets!! 👵🏻

name: Vacation Legs
description: Or sea legs from the water park? That didn't feel good but I did it

name: Snow Snake 🐍
description: 210' of vertical drop, $44 lift tickets and kids ski free  the kids are crushin it! + My first tow rope, damn those things are violent   Yes this is just social media now

name: Cobbles up shitz down
description: Crankin on stress and coffee. Lots of people out at 3 on a Friday. Doesn't anyone work? 🤪

name: Shosh's jaunt
description: Prepping for tomorrow's fundraiser run

name: Lunch Mountain Bike Ride
description: It was hot. We were hungry.

name: Post nap
description: Another run of 6am meetings but 20 min of early afternoon snooze reinvigorated me. Still wanted something easy so I took a mostly fire road route. Did ride the down Shitz which was cool, since the last time I tried that trail I was borrowing Joel's bike, had no idea how to ride, and walked down all the berms.

name: Afternoon Run
description: Played a couple more games of pickleball yesterday and while I'm not going to admit that I'm sore from it, it did seem like I could benefit from a shakeout run. 😂

name: Lunch Pickleball
description: Ate a pickle before and after 🥒

name: Afternoon Mountain Bike Ride
description: Connected a few trails with Joel. Forgot to start recording until we hit cobbles

name: Conquered my nemesis (a pile of rocks)
description: Finally made it through both small uphill rock gardens just east of the waterfall without coming off the bike! Woohoo!   I took an extended release Sudafed this morning and my heart was all aflutter in the afternoon. Went for the mentally easy route up the fire road and across the bridle paths. Side Hill was in surprisingly good shape for bombing down.  Also got to rock my "cloud" lenses for the full desert color look.

name: Desert temps
description: Dry and hot. Busy day on the trail. Tried to focus on the cadence uphill and only had to bail once  First time seeing duck pond without algae

name: Natick
description: Back to the mother ship to revisit my most run Strava segment of 2025

name: Boston
description: Andrea Staid keeping the pace honest

name: Black Widow
description: My second black trail. It was very cool. Views 10/10. Could see from Mt. Woodson to the ocean

name: Afternoon Mountain Bike Ride
description: A quick exploration. Very muddy. Very green.   People were training guard dogs 🐕

name: Bike path westward
description: That is boring and loud. Still rained out of the canyon though

name: Jan 1
description: Ran with Joel and my parents' neighbor. A quick sneak away from the gaggle of kids

name: Back in Black (Mountain)
description: Made it up most of the first big climb past the gate but then was tired the rest of the time 😅  Clear skies and ocean views  Nighthawk downhill is a never repeat tho

name: Xmas strut
description: Did not spot any secret service

name: Before the rains
description: Spotted: approximately 200 snails and one coyote

name: Sightseeing
description: Pretty relaxed ride, explored some trails behind the mesa which were meh. But nice views! As M would say "I see Black Mountain!”  Stopped to take a picture of the goat who is always on the shed, since I don't carry my phone on runs. But some old guys were feeding the goats so they were at the fence instead.
Unseasonably warm, even for here. Still wet on the south side of the creek

name: Made my first Strava segment
description: After yo-yo ing with some dudes biking up Powerlines, I spent the rest of the run pondering about what uphill grades would be faster to bike vs. run. So I added a running segment to compare with my bike times 😂   Conclusion: my bike PR on that section is faster than today's run, but on most of my rides, I was slower. Not surprising I guess since it's fairly steep, but I wonder where the tipping point is.

name: Black Mountain
description: Rocks, rocks, fighter jets, rocks, and a softball team on some kind of hike with full uniforms and gear.

name: New Tunnel who dis
description: Played Passenger Princess again and let Joel show me Tunnel 2. It's a cool, slower trail which was perfect because I was feeling slow mentally and physically

name: Back on the ground
description: Whew it was a little hotter and drier than I expected, but overall pretty pleasant. Bumped into some friends hiking in the canyon and ended up running a quarter mile with three 6-year-olds 😅

name: Flat stuff
description: My mind is toast after four 6am meetings this week and one 6:30, but I needed to get some movement in, so I just rode along the bottom of the canyon. Honestly east of our house is not that great. Also was totally blinded by the low angle sun heading home

name: Cobbles-Appendix-Cobbles
description: Perfect weather, felt fresh  Stymied by two steep uphill sections but otherwise felt fairly competent climbing . Tried to focus on cadence

name: Mission Trails
description: Getting my 🍑🦵🏻 by long climbs.  Very cool trail toward the end. Might choose a different start next time.

name: Ruts
description: why is uphill so technically difficult?

name: Back to the canyon
description: Trails finally dried out enough (ranger's orders) to use again so I am spared the boring roads. There was a strange group of silent stare-ers at the waterfall? Dunno

name: Family Ride around Lake Miramar!
description: Wooo first time riding with all four of us! Miriam did catch a lift on the cargo bike after 1.5 miles but Shoshana made it all the way around.   Not breaking any speed records (it is legitimately difficult to bike 4mph haha) but hey, we're out there.  Oh, and Joel Brooks rollerbladed!

name: Finding running buddies on Facebook
description: Slower than my default pace but she has two young kids, her husband went to my high school (didn't overlap), and is Jewish so I guess we're friends now

name: Silver Strand 12k
description: Not bad for being bent over a toilet bowl 12 hours ago (mysterious GI distress).  A friend convinced me to sign up but I mostly ran alone. Taiko drumming is a great motivator.  Update: Apparently placed second in my age group!

name: Morning Run
description: Did some strides after. Let's see if I stretch today

name: Miriam woke us up at 5am
description: Who doesn't love a time change?  I had one of those "trying to move my legs but can't" running dreams last night so I thought I'd try proving to myself that my legs weren't stuck. Went to the track and did 4x800 with 200 jog rest and then a 400 at the end. Just trying to snap out of the slowly plodding along vibe I've been riding for a while

name: Oof
description: Terribly timed run with respect to food, temperature, and work stress. Ugh. Well, I got out there  Splits are crazy because I was trying to learn "workout mode" on my watch

name: Hills are hard when biking also
description: After a lot of slipping around and one wipeout, it finally clicked that "mash pedals as hard as possible" is not the path to uphill success. (What, brute force doesn't solve my problems? Ugh). Tired now, nice ride overall though.

name: Same old (not so) boring route
description: My years of experience dealing with big feelings came in handy today when I... broke up a trail altercation?? I came up a hill to find a guy on a MTB arguing with a woman with a dog. Turned out the dog lunged at the guy and he got pissed and immediately started recording the woman, who looked terrified and kept saying she was just dog sitting. I got them to calm down by:  - Confirming that everyone was physically ok - Acknowledging both people's feelings  - Suggesting that instead of casting blame, we just accept what happened and try to learn from it - Asking the man to take a deep breath and just bike away  SOUND FAMILIAR, PARENTS???

name: Copycat route + some exploring
description: didn't feel like making choices so I basically just copied Joel Brooks's ride from yesterday. Tacked on some random side trails. Found a very nice view 🌄 and one orienteering waypoint ⛳

name: Oops double hill
description: I had a grand plan of dropping M off this morning and stopping at Black Mountain on the way home to run while it was still cool. But then someone scheduled a last minute meeting and I had do "work" and I ended up running in the afternoon sun. Thanks to some gentle encouragement from a B and Andrea Staid , I stuck with hills instead of wimping out. But I accidentally did an extra hill when what looked like a cut across, wasn't.  Didn't feel great out there, I think I'm just a really bad hill runner. Or maybe I need to start taking a multivitamin? Probably.... I should stretch.

name: High tide adventure
description: The original plan was to try to get 8 or 9 mi in but I misjudged how high the tide was (or maybe the beach and cliffs have evolved in 20 years?) and ended up getting wet. Took my socks and shoes off and walked for a bit before getting to the bottom of Broken Hills. Then I ran up the trail with shoes on but carrying my socks to try to let them dry out. Put the socks back on at the top, but then decided to turn around before N Torrey Pines Rd since the salt water on my clothes was starting to chafe. But hey, it was still really pretty! Guy Fleming on the way down because I couldn't resist.

name: Lunch Mountain Bike Ride
description: Getting more confident going fast downhill. Now I need to figure out how to do technical things uphill. Had to bail off the bike at one point 😅

name: The black and white goat always sleeps on top of the shed
description: That is an observation, not a metaphor

name: Eastward
description: Wanted to see how far east the canyon trail went, but it was way hotter than I expected and bad timing with meetings and meals. Anyway, there is more canyon

name: Lunch Mountain Bike Ride
description: Not sore anymore but still very fatigued. Thankfully improving skill can compensate for lack of muscle.  Thanks for tagging along for consecutive days, Joel Brooks !

name: Afternoon Run
description: Social run, trying to unlock the calves

name: Mt. Whitney from Whitney Portal and JMT
description: (No government edition.)  Fantastic day! Started at 3:15am and finished just before 7pm. We had amazing luck with the weather; two days ago there was snow/hail/lots of wind. We were treated to sun, refreshingly cool air, and amazing views the whole way (even in the dark! 🌌) and saw a couple of pikas. Definitely want to spend more time in the Sierras now that we're out here, hopefully with the kids!  We tried to concentrate on starting slow and felt strong up to Trail Camp. Then started to feel the altitude and the slog about halfway up the switchbacks. Slowed down and made sure to take breaks after that, and summited feeling strong! Then the long road down. Tried to goad Joel into jogging toward the end "just to mix up muscle groups" but alas.

name: A stroll from Cottonwood Lakes Trailhead
description: Started in a rain squall but then it was beautiful. 82 degrees at sea level and 42 at 10k ft

name: E-cargo bike adventure
description: Boldly tried to use the cargo bike (with kiddo) through a single track trail. Shoshana seemed to enjoy bouncing around and getting blasted by plants, so I guess it worked out. Had a long struggle to try to get the bike up a super steep incline, lol. Even walking it was iffy

name: Morning Run
description: Chillaxin, meeting all the cool dogs

name: Afternoon Mountain Bike Ride
description: Saw a bobcat, roadrunner, and a coyote! Getting a bit more confident with speed downhill. Unexpectedly launched myself over a bump but did not fall 😁  Matchy matchy outfit achieved

name: Mt. Woodson (and Potato Chip Rock)
description: Squeezing in a local hike. Thankful for cloud cover instead of the predicted 80 degree day.   Sure, you feel good about your fitness until you meet a Pomeranian at the top.

name: Mesa to Cobbles
description: Roughly traced the bike route from Tues. Super muggy after yesterday's rain

name: Afternoon Mountain Bike Ride
description: Learned some stuff, did not fall of side of mountain.  Forgot to unpause and missed recording the boring section

name: Praise the clouds
description: Nice long adventure. Saw a coyote and checked out two more creek crossings. Definitely a bit hillier on the southwest section

name: Morning Run
description: Even though I did already exercise a bunch this week, I really felt the need to go on a "regular" run. And it was indeed regular.

name: Anniversary Ride 🎊🥂
description: Bought myself a mountain bike as my gift to Joel Brooks for our anniversary. 🤣🤔Spent the actual day together at the DMV, so got our first ride together in today

name: Catching up
description: Ran with an old friend, explored the Lake Hodges area. Definitely a bunch of nice trails around!

name: Morning Trail Run
description: My parents had the kids overnight (so we could go see the Savannah Bananas!!) so Joel Brooks  and I took the opportunity to work out together.  Some guy on Mt. San Jacinto last month described trail running as "you run, and then when it gets too hard, you walk" so that's that we did

name: Lunch Mountain Bike Ride
description: Oops got in a little over my head and had to walk down  some twisty turny banked things. Then I got tired and hungry and hot and started to really slow down at the end. But it was a nice alternative to a run in the baking sun

name: Morning Run
description: Found a trail that is downhill on the way back! 🙌🏻  Also found a disc golf basket with putters in it (belonged to the park??) so I stopped to throw a few

name: Morning Run
description: Pros of heading west in the morning: running towards fog/cloud cover and not being blinded by the sun.  Cons of heading west generally: the way back is a subtle, soul crushing, persistent uphill

name: Exploring Black Mountain
description: Was hoping to go a little further but a meeting ran long. Nice cloud cover today

name: Post hike shakeout
description: Joel doesn't know what a shakeout is so I took him on one

name: Mt San Jacinto from Marion Trailhead
description: Absolutely gorgeous hike, really lucked out on the weather. Shade the whole way up and got back to car just as a thunderstorm was forming.  Quite a steep start, almost too steep even for this caterpillar.

name: It's a dry heat
description: I wasn't running through soup like New England, instead just getting blasted by stellar radiation.  Cooled off halfway through by dunking my head into the waterfall.  Finally found my trail shoes, wow what a difference

name: Post drop off run
description: This. run. had. everything. 🙏🏻: hard packed dirt, a historic house, river rocks, a moderate climb, a bearded man stacking rocks, loose sand, and lizards. (One of which I almost stepped on because it ran forward instead of sideways??)  A pretty hot one today but managed to find a lot of shade

name: Morning Run
description: Still sore and sluggish from the Black Mtn run (because I don't stretch or otherwise recover!!).  But I kept on keepin on and got a nice longer run in. Rolling hills kept me honest

name: Black Mountain via Miner's Loop
description: About 40% hike, 60% run. Fog burned off right as I rolled up but not too hot with the breeze, and was rewarded with nice views. Bunch of sharp rocks on the trail, so a little slow going to pick my way through. Only one wrong turn! (But I did stop to check AllTrails a bunch😅)

name: The mix up day
description: I swear I was already planning on stealing Joel's bike before I saw him come back from a run this morning.  Turns out the fire road is just as miserable on the bike as running. Did two loops of one single track trail which was fun.

name: Found Tunnel 4, lost the way out
description: Why is this place so confusing for me? Twisty and rocky through tunnel 4, I see why it's a cool MTB trail. I might need trail shoes though.  Saw a family of quail on the fire road! Kesavan Yogeswaran you gotta come visit and get your bird count up

name: Canyon Camp Commute
description: Well this sure is pleasant

name: Afternoon Run
description: Ran to the waterfall, crossed the creek, and back on the south side. A little trouble finding the crossing on the east end. 😂  Rough meeting+drop off schedule today and ended up running at 3pm. Was pretty hot and I hadn't eaten lunch yet. But I found enough shade and there was a consistent breeze

name: Del Mar Mesa Loop
description: This is a good loop. Thanks to Joel Brooks for scouting it on the bike. Not to get all 🧓,  but wow that used to be all dirt roads and strawberry fields. At least one farm still persists; ran by some goats standing on a shed.

name: Mesa attempt
description: Tried running up hill first toward Del Mar Mesa, but I hit a lot of "No Trespassing" signs so I wasn't sure where I could go. Bailed back through a neighborhood to the canyon. There is a VERY impressive yucca on Park Village Rd and Mannix. Went back for a picture, it's like 25 ft tall!

More canyon exploring. Saw a pack of 5 coyotes! They're much smaller than the mega-coyotes of Arlington, more like cattle-dog size. There was even a little pup. I respectfully let them cross ahead of me and proceeded on my way.  I'm still a pretty stiff morning runner, hopefully my body will adjust 🫤

name: Morning Run
description: Exploring side trails and figuring out where I can make loops. Still pretty tired and out of whack from the move (and the kids keep waking up at night).

name: Exploring with S and 🐶
description: A slow Shosh stroll. Saw a mule deer and a horse wearing horse sandals

name: Morning Run
description: Went east, crossed the creek.

name: New Home Course
description: Exactly 2 miles to the PQ waterfall! Water a little low right now.

name: See ya later, Massachusetts
description: Dropped the car off with the shipper and ran back to the Airbnb (plus an extra 2 mi)  Much nicer to run early in the AM and in the shade. Still a sweat fest.  See you on the flip side!

name: Miserable moving prep
description: Ran from the Airbnb back to meet Joel for house clean out. Straight up the service road in the heat

name: Omni Mt. Washington
description: MathWorks summer outing. I had a different idea in mind for a route but then decided to get lost on XC ski trails

name: Variation on a theme
description: West Farm downhill, meadows, a pass by the Res

name: Morning Run
description: Sweat sweat sweat

name: Midland City Forest 2
description: The posted trail map overestimates all the distances

name: Midland City Forest
description: 15 degrees cooler and way less humid. Lovely, shady run  I don't think I'm going to turn into a cold weather wimp after we move but I sure am going to whine at the slightest hint of humidity

name: Muggy in Michigan
description: Oof

name: Personal Arboretum Tour
description: Uncle Ira the docent!

name: 40 for 40
description: Garmin remembered my birthday!  Was able to sneak out before the temps crept up and saw 🐇🐇🐿️🐿️🐢🐸🐇🐇🐇🐇🐇🐇🕸️🕸️

name: Is this... Cool down? 🦋
description: Shoshana came to cheer which was great but also meant I answered infinite questions instead of cooling down

name: Somerville Road Runners 26x1 Leg 15, 6:27 split
description: Wow, way easier to run with people around than alone in the middle of the workday.  Got sucked into the hype and accidentally took off with a 90 sec first lap but thankfully it didn't tank me. Pretty pleased

name: Forgotten lunch
description: Had to drop off a lunch box. Also "had to" stop at Butternut Bakehouse

name: Lunch Run
description: Stopped at Fresh Pond on the way home from the dentist. It smelled really nice (flowers) but I ran into so many clouds of gnats 🪰   Still a bit stiff from the hike

name: Baldface Circle Trail
description: A pre-birthday adventure with Andrea Staid . A little spicy in some sections, but less spicy than the banh mi we hauled up for lunch (not pictured).   Thanks to the dads for watching the kids solo on Father's Day!  Edit: just found this hike on someone's "Terrifying 25" list for New England, so there's that https://www.newenglandwaterfalls.com/terrifying25.php

name: Mile Trial
description: Running a 26x1 relay with some coworkers next weekend so I wanted to test out how much it would suck. It's probably unhealthy how warming up on a track can just instill butterflies, even for something with no stakes.  Did the full 1609 meters 😁

name: Picking up the kids
description: Talking about Mega Mewtwo Y

name: Picking up the bike
description: All downhill, 60 F with no humidity. What a run

name: The worst timing choice
description: Post thunderstorm + sun out + general NYC swampiness = 🥵🥵🥵🥵🥵

name: A true morning run
description: My first early morning, pre-breakfast run in a long time. It's going to reach 91 today and the kids randomly woke up mega early and happy, so I figured I'd take advantage and beat the heat.  Lots of fish activity

name: Sneaking one in
description: Turns out we were supposed to be out of the house with Harry today so they could prep and take photos so Joel and I are working remote from the Res. 🤪  Sneaking in a short Whipple run and I guess I'm taking the rest of my meetings sweaty

name: Orienteering! 🧭
description: Work outing to learn orienteering. It's... Medium 😂

name: Lunch Run
description: Still mildly congested, but mostly sluggish. Maybe I should make more time in my life to stretch.

name: Escápe
description: I was acting like a real C- parent this morning so I figured I needed to get out for a breather. In my defense, the kids have woken us up by screaming at 6:15 three mornings in a row. 🥱🥱

name: Shoshana's Mile
description: S has been planning to wake up early and run 4 laps since Thursday and today was the big day!  Big snack break after lap 2, a walking lap 3, then a sprint home.  Strava is my social media now

name: Morning Run
description: Connected all my loops. Saw a baby snapping turtle. Joel says I shouldn't have touched it 🤷‍♀️

name: Unmotivated and muggy
description: Had to drag myself out the door. Rewarded with swamp conditions.  One day, I will appreciate the added fitness of having to end all my runs up a big hill, but today is not that day.

name: They said they wanted to go to a track!
description: The part of mother's day *with* my kids 🥰

name: Mother's Day Gift to Myself
description: I told Joel I wanted a tiramisu and first shift for a workout. There appears to be some cake box in the fridge 😁  A bit of a slog bc *someone's* gift to everyone was waking up twice in the night (guess who!) but great conditions so can't complain.  And solidarity with this turkey momma and her poults

name: An unexpected adventure
description: Originally was gonna just take Harry out for a short one but I then I let Miriam just pick where we went at every intersection. Recording only started halfway through

name: "Efficient" CRG
description: Didn't warm up or rest and stopped at the first sign of fatigue

name: Lunch Run
description: A poor night's sleep really made the Slyvia St hill miserable. Why is M so bad at sleeping??

name: Home Inspection
description: Checking out the trail around the corner from hopefully our new place!

name: Horseshit and a rattlesnake
description: Just a baby. One rattle.

name: Smite reunion!
description: Strava helped Claire Kearns-McCoy and I discover we were both in SD at the same time so we met up for a walk at Torrey Pines. Beautiful weather, as always 😂  Oops Claire and I forgot to take a picture together

name: Time zone shakeout
description: A little dip in the canyon. If a wog is a walk-jog then I'm calling this a hog: hike-jog  The kids woke up at 3:45 and 5:15, respectively, and have been riding the vacation hype train continuously since then ☠️ I can't believe it's only 10:30 right now

name: Morning Run
description: Took the day off to prep for travel so figured I'd run somewhere more exciting. Saw what looked like adaptive mountain biking which was cool  Legs felt like bricks, I guess more breakfast next time?

name: A long, meandering pick up
description: Daycare, argue over seat choice, playground, argue over seat choice, giggle like nothing ever happened

name: Errand run
description: Dropped the car off for service, ran around, picked up bike from the shop

name: Ode to Paul Revere?
description: Was a day late for all the Minutemen but it seemed thematic to run to Lexington.  Net uphill after dropping a bike off on Mass Ave 🥵  Good luck tomorrow a B , Margaret Leibovic, and Calvin French-Owen !

name: Morning Run
description: Look at me I ran before work! (Kids are off school so no mad scramble 😂)

name: Lunch Run
description: A reattempt of a route I tried when it was snowy. I did make it to Wellesley College this time, but I'm not sure if the slog down Bacon is worth it.  Maybe I'll give it one more try without a breakfast burrito sitting in my belly

name: Afternoon Run
description: Ran to Trader Joe's. Wogged home with chocolate chips, hummus, and 2 lb of brown sugar 😂

name: Cross Fells Trail Jaunt

name: Western Greenway from Rock Meadows
description: Whew. That was more trail-like (i.e. difficult) than I anticipated from the map. All the rocks, roots, twists, and small hills made it difficult to get into any kind of rhythm... but also made it a really nice run. In any case, I did it! 10 mi to cap off my 2024 Return to Fitness. (Definitely Return to Fitness and not a midlife crisis, despite what the Boston Globe says about Spartan Races. 😂)  === Year end recap ===   R2F actually started in late 2023 when I hired a personal trainer through my work's gym.  I hadn't done any serious exercise since my first pregnancy and I was tired of feeling frustrated about being out of shape. For about 6 months we met roughly weekly for a 45 minute lifting session. I started off the series by insisting that I really only had time for one workout a week with the kids' schedules. He respected that and also very gently dropped hints like "well, what if you went for a short run one of the other days of the week?" Slowly I started to feel more functional again.  Over the summer, also through the work gym, I signed up and trained for a 5K Spartan Race. The trainers sent out 5 days/wk worth of workouts, which was intimidating at first, but they were realistic. Lifts that took 30 min or less, or sometimes just 20 min of mobility work. Did the Spartan Race in the pouring rain and felt great.  Finally, in the fall, I convinced Dre to convince me to sign up for a 5 mile trail race. 5 miles was longer than I had run before getting pregnant with S, and on trails. I decided that consistency was the best path to being ready. So I bought a Garmin and told myself that I had to run every other day, regardless of how short. Interestingly, the Garmin told me that my normal loops from home were much shorter than I had estimated 😁 That adventure went pretty well and from there I decided that I just wanted to be able to do longer runs again. So, starting in late November, I planned out a 6 mile route with a goal to extend 1 mile per week, trying to get to 10 by the end of the year, keeping my weekday mileage at the same (low) consistency. A few illnesses and sleep deprived nights threatened whether I'd make it, but I squeaked it in!  Going into 2025, I should probably add some lifting back in, giving how much I struggle on hills of any size. This route is probably a good test piece for that. 😊

name: Across 3 - Running Edition
description: Did it! House-to-house run. Joel took the kids to my parents' house and I ran there.   A really nice route along Del Mar Mesa. Uphill for the first 2 miles, rolling hills/net downhill for the next 3, then (sadly) a finish with an uphill mile, including a march/walk up Telephone Pole Hill. Can't believe we used to do hill repeats up that.  Nice to know that if the AI apocalypse happens and we run out of gas and electricity, I can still get to my parents' house 😂
